skill.name: quill
skill.description: Distills writing styles from author names or sample texts, then creates reusable writing style cards for rewriting and style experiments.
skill.summary: Create reusable writing style cards from text samples or author references.
skill.author: sevenwoood

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and entropy indicators, but review found they are documentation examples and Markdown formatting rather than executable code. No prompt injection attempt, credential access, data exfiltration path, executable script, or hidden runtime behavior was found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Documentation Installation Commands Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The command-looking content is inside Markdown installation examples for gh, npx, and git clone. The skill package contains Markdown instructions and assets, with no executable scripts or code path that runs these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is low because the lines are fenced Markdown examples, not runtime code. Review found no executable file or script that invokes shell commands.

low_findings.title: Manual Skill Directory Paths Flagged as Hidden File Access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hidden home-directory paths are installation destinations for common AI tool skill folders. They do not read secrets, traverse files, or instruct the AI to access private user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is very low because the paths appear only in user-facing manual installation documentation. No evidence found of automated filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Entropy Heuristic on Text Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis marked several Markdown files as high entropy, but file inspection identified UTF-8 text documentation and examples, not binary payloads, encrypted blobs, or obfuscated code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Threat confidence is very low because the flagged files are readable Markdown. No encoded payload, packed script, or hidden binary content was found in the text files.

content.value_statement: Writers often struggle to describe a style clearly enough to reuse it. Quill analyzes examples or author names and turns the result into a practical style card for rewriting.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes writing style across vocabulary, syntax, rhetoric, perspective, rhythm, and narrative structure.
Creates reusable writing style cards from author names or provided sample texts.
Blends multiple styles with user-confirmed ratios for each major style dimension.
Rewrites text at light, medium, or deep intensity while preserving the intended meaning.
Adds revision notes that explain vocabulary, sentence, rhetoric, and style changes.
Applies de-AI writing checks to reduce template-like phrasing and uniform sentence patterns.
limitations: Style analysis is qualitative and depends on the quality and amount of source material.
The skill does not verify copyright status or permissions for user-provided text.
Author-name distillation depends on the base model knowledge available in the active AI tool.
Generated output can imitate style, but it should not be presented as work by the original author.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Style Card
use_cases.description: Feed several original essays or stories and generate a style card that helps keep future drafts consistent.

use_cases.title: Practice Literary Style Analysis
use_cases.description: Study how vocabulary, rhythm, imagery, and point of view shape a recognizable writing voice.

use_cases.title: Unify Brand Copy Tone
use_cases.description: Distill examples of approved copy and use the resulting style card to rewrite new marketing drafts.

prompt_templates.title: Start Style Distillation
prompt_templates.prompt: Start Quill. I want to distill the style of these writing samples. Ask me for anything missing before creating the style card.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Style Card from Samples
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the following three texts as one writing style. Create a reusable style card with vocabulary, sentence rhythm, rhetoric, perspective, and rewrite rules.

prompt_templates.title: Blend Two Styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Blend these two styles. Show the main conflicts by dimension, ask me for ratio choices, then create a combined style card.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite with Controlled Intensity
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the current style card to this draft at medium intensity. Keep the core meaning, enable narrative structure if useful, and include revision notes.

output_examples.input: A user provides several prose samples and asks for a reusable style.
output_examples.output: Quill returns a style card with vocabulary patterns, sentence rhythm, rhetoric rules, perspective guidance, timing structure, and anti-template checks.

output_examples.input: A user asks to blend two author-inspired styles.
output_examples.output: Quill presents a dimension-by-dimension blend table, asks for ratio choices, and generates a combined writing style card.

output_examples.input: A user asks to rewrite a paragraph with an existing style card.
output_examples.output: Quill rewrites the paragraph and adds concise notes explaining changes to word choice, syntax, rhetoric, and style intensity.

best_practices: Provide three to five representative samples when possible.
State any forbidden phrases, topics, or imitation boundaries before rewriting.
Label generated text as simulated style when it references a real author.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to impersonate a living or historical author.
Do not feed copyrighted text unless you have the right to use it.
Do not expect precise quantitative analysis from very short samples.

faq.question: Does Quill copy an author word for word?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to imitate style patterns, not reproduce exact source text.

faq.question: Can I use my own writing samples?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can analyze your samples and create a reusable style card for future drafts.

faq.question: How many samples work best?
faq.answer: Three to five short or medium samples usually give better results than one short passage.

faq.question: Can it blend more than one style?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare style dimensions and ask for blend ratios before generating a combined card.

faq.question: Does it run code or install tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill files are documentation and prompt instructions. Installation commands appear only in README examples.

faq.question: Is this safe for published writing?
faq.answer: Use clear disclosure when simulating a named style, and avoid plagiarism or false attribution.
